questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The zero vector has a magnitude of 0.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The magnitude of a vector is defined as the square root of the dot product of the vector with itself.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The zero vector has a magnitude of 0 as it is the result of the dot product of any vector with itself, which equals 0.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The magnitude of a vector is always non-negative.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The magnitude of a vector is defined as the square root of the dot product of the vector with itself.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The magnitude of a vector is always non-negative as the square root of any non-negative number is non-negative.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The position vector of a point is unique.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The position vector of a point is the vector from the origin to the point.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The position vector of a point is unique as it is defined as the vector from the origin to the point, which is unique for every point in space.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The vector joining the points A(a, b, c) and B(x, y, z) is given by <x-a, y-b, z-c>.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The vector joining two points A and B is given by the difference of the position vectors of B and A.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The vector joining two points A and B is indeed given by the difference of the position vectors of B and A, which is <x-a, y-b, z-c>.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The position vector of a point P(x, y, z) is given by <x, y, z>.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The position vector of a point P is the vector from the origin to the point P.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The position vector of a point P(x, y, z) is indeed given by <x, y, z> as it is the vector from the origin to the point P.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The vector <a, b, c> is equal to the vector <a, b, c>, which is parallel to vector <a, b, c>.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Two vectors are equal if and only if they have the same magnitude and direction.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is correct as two vectors are indeed equal if and only if they have the same magnitude and direction.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The vector <a, b, c> is not equal to the vector <c, b, a>.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Two vectors are equal if and only if they have the same magnitude and direction.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is correct as vectors <a, b, c> and <c, b, a> have different directions and are therefore not equal.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The vector <a, b, c> is parallel to the vector <c, b, a>.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Two vectors are parallel if and only if they are scalar multiples of each other.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is true as vectors <a, b, c> and <c, b, a> are parallel but the reason given is not the correct explanation as they are not scalar multiples of each other.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The vector <a, b, c> is not a unit vector.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A unit vector is a vector with magnitude 1.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is correct as the vector <a, b, c> is not necessarily a unit vector, unless its magnitude is 1.
